from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of Triples about an astronaut (or related entities) into a
    single, readable sentence.  The function uses a small set of handcrafted
    templates for the supported predicates and joins the resulting clauses
    with commas and a final “and”.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Basic templates for the most common astronaut predicates.
    # Each template receives (subject, object) and returns a clause string.
    # Helper to clean object strings (remove surrounding quotes)
    def _clean(val: str) -> str:
        return val.strip('"').strip("'")

    # Mapping of countries to common demonyms for better readability
    _demonyms = {
        "United States": "American",
        "United Kingdom": "British",
        "Canada": "Canadian",
        "Russia": "Russian",
        "China": "Chinese",
    }

    # Predicates that describe a relationship where the object is a type belonging to the subject
    _entity_predicates = {"fossil", "bird", "gemstone", "mascot", "award", "ribbonAward"}

    templates = {
        # Astronaut personal data
        "almaMater": lambda s, o: f"studied at {_clean(o)}",
        "birthDate": lambda s, o: f"was born on {_clean(o)}",
        "birthName": lambda s, o: f"was originally named {_clean(o)}",
        "birthPlace": lambda s, o: f"was born in {_clean(o)}",
        "dateOfRetirement": lambda s, o: f"retired on {_clean(o)}",
        "occupation": lambda s, o: f"was a {_clean(o).lower()}",
        "status": lambda s, o: f"is now {_clean(o).lower()}",
        "timeInSpace": lambda s, o: f"spent {_clean(o)} minutes in space",
        "selectedByNasa": lambda s, o: f"was selected by NASA in {_clean(o)}",
        "deathDate": lambda s, o: f"died on {_clean(o)}",
        # Improved nationality handling with demonyms
        "nationality": lambda s, o: _demonyms.get(_clean(o), _clean(o)),
        "servedAsChiefOfTheAstronautOfficeIn": lambda s, o: f"served as chief of the Astronaut Office in {_clean(o)}",
        "title": lambda s, o: f"held the title \"{_clean(o)}\"",
        "ribbonAward": lambda s, o: f"received the {_clean(o)} ribbon",
        # Mission‑related predicates – note the direction of the statement
        "mission": lambda s, o: f"{s} was a crew member on {_clean(o)}",
        "operator": lambda s, o: f"{s} was operated by {_clean(o)}",
        "backupPilot": lambda s, o: f"{_clean(o)} was a backup pilot for {s}",
        "commander": lambda s, o: f"{_clean(o)} was the commander of {s}",
        "crewMembers": lambda s, o: f"{_clean(o)} was a crew member of {s}",
        # New predicates for political/representative relations
        "senators": lambda s, o: f"{_clean(o)} is a senator from {s}",
        "representative": lambda s, o: f"{_clean(o)} is the representative of {s}",
        # Miscellaneous
        "alternativeName": lambda s, o: f"was also known as {_clean(o)}",
        "awards": lambda s, o: f"has {_clean(o)} awards",
        "award": lambda s, o: f"received the award {_clean(o)}",
        "deathPlace": lambda s, o: f"died in {_clean(o)}",
        # Generic handling for entity predicates (e.g., fossil, bird, gemstone, mascot)
        "default": lambda s, o: f"{s} {triple.predicate.replace('_', ' ')} {_clean(o)}",
    }

    # Group triples by subject to produce one fluent sentence per subject.
    from collections import defaultdict

    subject_groups = defaultdict(list)
    for triple in triples:
        subject_groups[triple.subject].append(triple)

    sentences = []
    for subj, group in subject_groups.items():
        demonym = None
        clause_parts = []
        for triple in group:
            # Handle nationality specially: store demonym and skip adding clause
            if triple.predicate == "nationality":
                demonym = templates["nationality"](triple.subject, triple.object)
                continue
            tmpl = templates.get(triple.predicate, templates["default"])
            clause = tmpl(triple.subject, triple.object)
            # Remove leading subject if the template already included it.
            if clause.lower().startswith(subj.lower()):
                clause = clause[len(subj):].strip()
            clause_parts.append(clause)

        # Build a single sentence for this subject.
        if clause_parts:
            # Build subject phrase with optional demonym
            subject_phrase = subj
            if demonym:
                subject_phrase = f"The {demonym} {subj}"
            # If there is more than one clause, join with commas and a final “and”.
            if len(clause_parts) > 1:
                body = f"{subject_phrase} " + ", ".join(clause_parts[:-1]) + ", and " + clause_parts[-1]
            else:
                body = f"{subject_phrase} {clause_parts[0]}"
            # Capitalise first character and add a period.
            sentences.append(body[0].upper() + body[1:] + ".")

    # Combine sentences for different subjects with a space.
    return " ".join(sentences)

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of Triples about an astronaut (or related entities) into a
    single, readable sentence.  The function uses a small set of handcrafted
    templates for the supported predicates and joins the resulting clauses
    with commas and a final “and”.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Basic templates for the most common astronaut predicates.
    # Each template receives (subject, object) and returns a clause string.
    # Helper to clean object strings (remove surrounding quotes)
    def _clean(val: str) -> str:
        return val.strip('"').strip("'")

    # Mapping of countries to common demonyms for better readability
    _demonyms = {
        "United States": "American",
        "United Kingdom": "British",
        "Canada": "Canadian",
        "Russia": "Russian",
        "China": "Chinese",
    }

    # Predicates that describe a relationship where the object is a type belonging to the subject
    _entity_predicates = {"fossil", "bird", "gemstone", "mascot", "award", "ribbonAward"}

    templates = {
        # Astronaut personal data
        "almaMater": lambda s, o: f"studied at {_clean(o)}",
        "birthDate": lambda s, o: f"was born on {_clean(o)}",
        "birthName": lambda s, o: f"was originally named {_clean(o)}",
        "birthPlace": lambda s, o: f"was born in {_clean(o)}",
        "dateOfRetirement": lambda s, o: f"retired in {_clean(o)}",
        "occupation": lambda s, o: f"was a {_clean(o).lower()}",
        "status": lambda s, o: f"is {_clean(o).lower()}",
        "timeInSpace": lambda s, o: f"spent {_clean(o)} minutes in space",
        "selectedByNasa": lambda s, o: f"was selected by NASA in {_clean(o)}",
        "deathDate": lambda s, o: f"died on {_clean(o)}",
        # Improved nationality handling with demonyms
        "nationality": lambda s, o: (
            f"is an {_demonyms.get(_clean(o), _clean(o))}"
            if _demonyms.get(_clean(o))
            else f"has nationality {_clean(o)}"
        ),
        "servedAsChiefOfTheAstronautOfficeIn": lambda s, o: f"served as chief of the Astronaut Office in {_clean(o)}",
        "title": lambda s, o: f"held the title \"{_clean(o)}\"",
        "ribbonAward": lambda s, o: f"received the {_clean(o)} ribbon",
        # Mission‑related predicates – note the direction of the statement
        "mission": lambda s, o: f"{s} was a crew member on {_clean(o)}",
        "operator": lambda s, o: f"{s} was operated by {_clean(o)}",
        "backupPilot": lambda s, o: f"{_clean(o)} was a backup pilot for {s}",
        "commander": lambda s, o: f"{_clean(o)} was the commander of {s}",
        "crewMembers": lambda s, o: f"{_clean(o)} was a crew member of {s}",
        # New predicates for political/representative relations
        "senators": lambda s, o: f"{_clean(o)} is a senator from {s}",
        "representative": lambda s, o: f"{_clean(o)} is the representative of {s}",
        # Miscellaneous
        "alternativeName": lambda s, o: f"was also known as {_clean(o)}",
        "awards": lambda s, o: f"earned {_clean(o)} awards",
        "award": lambda s, o: f"received the award {_clean(o)}",
        "deathPlace": lambda s, o: f"died in {_clean(o)}",
        # Generic handling for entity predicates (e.g., fossil, bird, gemstone, mascot)
        "default": lambda s, o: f"{s} {triple.predicate.replace('_', ' ')} {_clean(o)}",
    }

    # Group triples by subject to produce one fluent sentence per subject.
    from collections import defaultdict

    subject_groups = defaultdict(list)
    for triple in triples:
        subject_groups[triple.subject].append(triple)

    sentences = []
    for subj, group in subject_groups.items():
        clause_parts = []
        for triple in group:
            tmpl = templates.get(triple.predicate, templates["default"])
            clause = tmpl(triple.subject, triple.object)
            # Remove leading subject if the template already included it.
            if clause.lower().startswith(subj.lower()):
                clause = clause[len(subj):].strip()
            clause_parts.append(clause)

        # Build a single sentence for this subject.
        if clause_parts:
            # If there is more than one clause, join with commas and a final “and”.
            if len(clause_parts) > 1:
                body = f"{subj} " + ", ".join(clause_parts[:-1]) + ", and " + clause_parts[-1]
            else:
                body = f"{subj} {clause_parts[0]}"
            # Capitalise first character and add a period.
            sentences.append(body[0].upper() + body[1:] + ".")

    # Combine sentences for different subjects with a space.
    return " ".join(sentences)

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
